explanation: This command simulates a malicious rundll32.exe invocation to open a UDL file as described in the Sigma rule. It includes the required elements: rundll32.exe, oledb32.dll, OpenDSLFile, and a UDL file in the Downloads directory.
This command is a simplified version of the first, omitting quotes around the UDL file path. It still matches the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command adds redirection to suppress output, which is a common obfuscation technique. It still includes the required elements to match the Sigma rule.
This command uses a pipeline to filter output, which is a safe mutation technique. It still includes the required elements to match the Sigma rule.
This command uses an environment variable to store the UDL file path, which is a safe mutation technique. It still includes the required elements to match the Sigma rule.
